title: Rootless ipv6 network init failure
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

When launching a container with a network specified in rootless, the container CNI fails.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create podman network with IPv6 (taken from Kubernetes `kind`)
```yaml
{
   "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
   "name": "kind",
   "plugins": [
      {
         "type": "bridge",
         "bridge": "cni-podman0",
         "isGateway": true,
         "ipMasq": true,
         "hairpinMode": true,
         "ipam": {
            "type": "host-local",
            "routes": [
               {
                  "dst": "::/0"
               },
               {
                  "dst": "0.0.0.0/0"
               }
            ],
            "ranges": [
               [
                  {
                     "subnet": "fc00:f853:ccd:e793::/64",
                     "gateway": "fc00:f853:ccd:e793::1"
                  }
               ],
               [
                  {
                     "subnet": "10.88.2.0/24",
                     "gateway": "10.88.2.1"
                  }
               ]
            ]
         }
      },
      {
         "type": "portmap",
         "capabilities": {
            "portMappings": true
         }
      },
      {
         "type": "firewall",
         "backend": ""
      },
      {
         "type": "tuning"
      },
      {
         "type": "dnsname",
         "domainName": "dns.podman",
         "capabilities": {
            "aliases": true
         }
      }
   ]
}
```
2. Launch example kindst node image `podman run -it --net cni-podman-1 docker.io/kindest/node:v1.21.2`



**Describe the results you received:**
Container fails to initialize CNI
```
Error: error configuring network namespace for container 634a40cd3f28a8c8a5d011bebedd981ace142cc2ace7e91e2ca27ed02f24f56b: error adding pod reverent_jang_reverent_jang to CNI network "kind": failed to list chains: running [/usr/bin/ip6tables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 3: modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'ip6_tables': Operation not permitted
ip6tables v1.8.7 (legacy): can't initialize ip6tables table `nat': Table does not exist (do you need to insmod?)
Perhaps ip6tables or your kernel needs to be upgraded.
```

When I try running the command manually:

```zsh
/usr/bin/ip6tables -t nat -S --wait
Fatal: can't open lock file /run/xtables.lock: Permission denied
```

**WORKAROUND:**

Remove all IPv6 components from the notwork config allows the rootless pod to create

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

store:
  configFile: /home/mbagnara/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:

        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.6
        FUSE library version 3.10.4
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ pacman -Q podman
podman 3.2.2-2
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Can you run `sudo modprobe ip6_tables` and try again. I think the kernel module is not loaded.
That works. I added a PR to the upstream project to clarify this https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind/pull/2388/files
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
